title: processkit-cli JSONL lifecycle-event stream (v1)
description: One line of processkit-cli's `--jsonl` lifecycle-event stream. This is the machine-readable mirror of the normative prose in docs/schema.md; docs/schema.md is the source of truth on any discrepancy. Each event object shares a common envelope (`schema_version`, `time`, `event`) plus fields specific to its `event` type tag. Validated against `fixtures/schema/v1/events.jsonl` (the golden fixture) by `tests/events.rs`.

time.description: Emission time, RFC 3339 UTC, millisecond precision (`…Z`).

member.description: One entry of a members_snapshot. Only `pid` is populated today; the enriched fields are always present but explicitly null until ProcessKit ships members_info() (docs/schema.md, 'Enriched member fields').

ppid.description: Parent pid; null until the enriched member fields ship.

name.description: Executable name; null until the enriched member fields ship.

start_time.description: Process start time; null until the enriched member fields ship.

commandInfo.description: The command a run executed, in its redaction-aware v1 shape (docs/schema.md, 'Command redaction').

redacted.description: true by default; false only under --argv-raw.

argv.description: The raw argv, present only when redacted is false; null otherwise.

argv_sha256.description: Lowercase-hex SHA-256 fingerprint of argv; filled on every run regardless of redaction (docs/schema.md, 'Fingerprint').

hint.description: Worker-shape hint for a recognized argv (e.g. msbuild_node_reuse), else null (docs/schema.md, 'Hint classifier').

path.description: The file the stream was written to.

bytes.description: Full byte counter; exceeds the file size when truncated or a write failed.

sha256.description: Lowercase-hex SHA-256 of the bytes actually written to path.

truncated.description: true when the stream outran the per-stream capture ceiling.

write_error.description: true when a file write failed part-way through the stream.

runStarted.description: The run has begun: the child is spawned into the container.

run_id.description: The --run-id value, or a generated run-<pid>-<unix_nanos>.

root_pid.description: The root child's PID; null if the backend exposed none.

cwd.description: The child's working directory; null if it could not be resolved.

membersSnapshot.description: A point-in-time snapshot of the container's members.

rootExited.description: The root child exited on its own.

code.description: The exit code for exited; null otherwise.

signal.description: The signal number for a Unix signalled death; null otherwise.

members_before.description: The tree size (member count) about to be reaped.

cleanupFinished.description: Container teardown finished (after the hard kill).

soft_terminate.description: The soft-stop tier for a runner-imposed ending; null on the natural-exit path.

limitHit.description: A configured ProcessKit resource limit was exceeded or could not be applied. Reserved for v1 shape-pinning; not emitted at runtime in this version.

limit.description: Which limit, e.g. processes, memory, cpu.

timeout.description: The --timeout deadline elapsed while the child was still running.

grace_ms.description: The --grace window, ms; null if unset.

cancelled.description: The run was cancelled and torn down through the shared soft-stop -> grace -> hard-kill path.

source.description: ctrl_c for a local interactive Ctrl-C; control_cancel for a control-plane cancel.

grace_ms.description: The --grace window, ms; null if unset.

killed.description: The run was killed by a control-plane kill command: an immediate hard kill of the whole tree, with no soft stop and no grace.

spawnFailed.description: The program could not be started (not found, not executable, bad --cwd): the child never ran.

containerFailed.description: Creating the container, joining the child to it, or handing the terminal to an interactive child failed.

phase.description: create (the container could not be created), attach (the launch into it failed), or foreground (handing the terminal to an interactive child failed after it had already spawned).

runnerExit.description: The terminal event of every run: the exact code the runner process returns.

code.description: The exit code the runner process returns (child's code, or a runner-band code).

child_code.description: The child's own exit code when it exited on its own; null for a runner-imposed ending or a child that never produced one.

outputCaptured.description: Bounded stdout/stderr capture finished. Emitted only when run was given --capture-dir.
